D3 Chemistry L3 Apply Reference

pH Indicators — Two Colours Through One pKa

an acid-base indicator is a weak acid whose protonated and deprotonated forms have different chromophores; at pH = pKa they coexist 1:1 and the colour is the mixture

A pH indicator HIn ⇌ H⁺ + In⁻ has two forms with different absorption spectra. The Henderson-Hasselbalch equation pH = pKa + log([In⁻]/[HIn]) tells how the equilibrium responds. Below pKa-1 the colour is solidly the acid form; above pKa+1 the base form; in between, a transition through the mixed colour.

Pick the indicator and drag the pH. The swatch interpolates between acid and base colours; the table on the right shows the three classic indicators with their pKa values.
